results.title: Malpractice
results.brief_description: Malpractice is an emotional portrayal of a family’s efforts to come to terms with the result of medical malpractice; and a mother’s need to find out the truth. While most of the characters in this dramatic docu-drama are portrayed by actors, some hospital staff play themselves. The members of the legal profession are all practising solicitors and barristers. The findings of the tribunal are not scripted, but determined by the presentation of the case.

results.title: Malice in wonderland
results.creator_credit: Gus Trikonis, Jay Benson and ITC Productions
results.brief_description: The story of one of Hollywood’s most memorable and often bitter rivalries between Hedda Hopper (Jane Alexander) and Louella Parsons (Elizabeth Taylor). This made for television docudrama, based on the book “Hedda and Louella” by George Eells, is full of drama and comedy as these two very powerful women vie with each other to get the latest gossip or scandal in the glitzy Hollywood world of stars and stardom. Cast also includes Richard Dysart, Joyce Van Patten, Jon Cypher.

results.title: Malayan University
results.creator_credit: Osman Shamsudin and Malayan Film Unit
results.brief_description: The University of Malaya is young, but its growing reputation has already fully justified its foundation, in 1949, with full university status. It has faculties of Arts, Science and Medicine. The film shows the work being done in these and the wide range of cultural activities available to its students as well as their formal studies.

results.title: Malayan seashore
results.creator_credit: Tom Hodge and Malayan Film Unit
results.brief_description: The rich variety of delicate living organisms which abounds on the Malayan seashore, on sandy beaches, coral reefs and in mangrove swamps. The fascination lies in the various forms of sea life left by the receding tide and the strange creatures found above as well as below the surface.

results.title: Malayan rubber
results.creator_credit: Educational Foundation for Visual Aids, Malayan Enterprise and World Wide Pictures
results.brief_description: Growing of rubber in Malaya. This film, after an introductory historical sequence showing the establishment of rubber plantations, shows the salient features of life and work on a rubber plantation in Malaya.

results.title: Malaya: land of tin and rubber
results.creator_credit: Milan Herzog and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: A survey of life in rain forests of the Malay Peninsula, showing production of latex on a rubber plantation, mining and processing of tin ore, and activities on the island of Singapore. Discusses Malaya’s position in the area of world trade and reviews contributions of Chinese, Malayas, Indians, and Europeans to present-day culture.

results.title: Malaya
results.brief_description: A short impression of Malaya and its peoples, the film opens with scenes on the island of Penang, then tin mining near Kuala Lumpur is shown, with the remaining section of the film being concerned with Singapore.

results.title: Malawi: two young men
results.creator_credit: Max Reid, Churchill Films and Dimension Films
results.brief_description: After completing school, two friends are faced with the problem of finding a place in Malawian society. They are shown in an unsuccessful attempt to become middlemen, buying and smoking fish, then selling them at an inland market. Failing, they go to a city where they spend days seeking work. Unemployment, high cost of living and unfriendly people sends one of the two back to his village.

results.title: Malawi: the women
results.creator_credit: Max Reid, Churchill Films and Dimension Films
results.brief_description: The varying lifestyles of Malawian women are revealed through sequences showing them at work in a village, a city, and the suburbs of a city. Two women voice their views about their respective lives and these reflect the concerns of many at the contrasts of village and city life.

results.title: Making yourself understood: introduction to communication
results.creator_credit: John T. Bobbitt and Encyclopaedia Britannica Films
results.brief_description: Language written or spoken, is our principle tool of communication. Essentials for communication - communicator, content, medium, audience, response. Knowing the type of audience being addressed is essential to getting your message across, and to getting the response you desire.
